title: Swansson to Ferrari, Cachinho to Haas for Bahrain and Jeddah in Temporary Swap
meta: Published: 09 April 2026 | By Isabella Rossi, Breaking News & Live Reporter | F1CC News
caption: Ferrari and Haas have agreed a two‑race driver exchange.
body:
LONDON — Ferrari and Haas have agreed a temporary driver swap for the next two rounds of the 2026 F1CC season. Riley Swansson will join Ferrari for the Bahrain and Saudi Arabian Grands Prix, while Afonso Cachinho will move to Haas for the same period.

The agreement, confirmed by both teams on Thursday morning, is effective immediately. Swansson will drive the second Ferrari alongside championship leader Eetu Väisänen in Bahrain this weekend and in Jeddah the following week. Cachinho will partner George Konstantaras at Haas.

Cachinho has endured a torrid start to his Ferrari career. The Portuguese driver has scored zero points across the opening four rounds, has accumulated six penalty points and a formal warning on his FIA racing licence, and has been eliminated in Q1 at three of the four race weekends. Team Principal Randy Belacik had publicly warned after Shanghai that results needed to improve.

Swansson, in contrast, has impressed in limited machinery. The Australian has scored two points for Haas from 15th and 9th‑place finishes, consistently outpacing his qualifying positions. He is understood to have been recommended to Ferrari by senior figures within the driver academy.

Väisänen, who will partner Swansson in Bahrain, gave a brief reaction to the move. "Yeah, I like Afonso and I do hope the best for him," the championship leader said. "But I stand by the team's decision."

Neither Ferrari nor Haas has commented on whether the arrangement could become permanent. The swap covers only the Bahrain and Saudi Arabian rounds. Both drivers will then return to their original teams for the remainder of the season unless further changes are announced.

Ferrari currently lead the constructors' championship with 64 points. Haas are tenth with 4 points.

What it means: Cachinho has been given a brief reprieve away from the Ferrari spotlight. Swansson gets an unexpected opportunity in championship‑winning machinery. The next two races will shape the driver market conversation for the rest of 2026.

Last updated: 10:15 BST, 09 April 2026
